# LeakSanitizer suppressions (issue #54) — the documented M.5 process-lifetime
# allocations. The compiler deliberately never frees its own working memory (arena-less
# by design; the OS reclaims it at exit — see TEKO_MASTER_PLAN.md W7/S1: the S1 arena is
# a RUNTIME seam for generated programs, the compiler seam stays on libc). Everything
# below was collected EMPIRICALLY from `teko test . --coverage` under
# ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=1 on ubuntu/clang (all leak stacks' first project frame).
#
# Anything NOT allocated through these sites (raw malloc/strdup/fopen in src/**) still
# fails the ASan+LSan lane — that is the point of the lane.

# src/core.h compiler seam (static inline; visible as real frames in Debug builds)
leak:tk_alloc
leak:tk_realloc0
leak:tk_alloc_copy

# runtime string helpers (teko_rt.c / rt string surface) used by the compiler+VM with
# process-lifetime results
leak:tk_str_concat
leak:tk_str_of_bytes
leak:tk_str_slice
leak:tk_one_byte
leak:tk_u64_to_str
leak:tk_i64_to_str
leak:tk_ftoa
leak:tk_rt_str_from_utf8
